# Amygdala — LLM Context

> Amygdala is an open-source agentic SOC analyst built by Supernerve AI.
> Repository: https://github.com/supernerve-ai/amygdala
> License: MIT

## Summary

Amygdala is an autonomous Security Operations Center (SOC) analyst that connects to Splunk via MCP (Model Context Protocol) using token-based authentication. It pulls security alerts via SPL queries and feeds them into Foundation-Sec-1.1-8B — Splunk's security-specialized large language model — for threat classification and severity triage.

Based on triage output, Amygdala spawns investigation sub-agents that correlate related events, trace attacker lateral movement, and enrich context from surrounding log data. Final output is a structured incident report (JSON) plus a human-readable summary, pushed to Slack or any webhook.

No human is needed in the loop unless the escalation threshold is hit.

## Architecture

Pipeline: Alert Ingestion → Triage (Foundation-Sec LLM) → Investigation (Sub-Agents) → Report Builder → Output (Slack/Webhooks)

## Key Components

- AlertIngestor: Fetches alerts from Splunk via MCP server
- TriageAgent: Evaluates severity using Foundation-Sec-1.1-8B model
- InvestigateAgent: Correlates events, checks IOCs, builds timelines
- ReportBuilder: Generates structured JSON + human-readable reports
- OutputHandler: Delivers reports to Slack or webhook endpoints
- MCPClient: HTTP client for Splunk MCP server communication

## Tech Stack

- Language: Python 3.12+
- Async Framework: asyncio + httpx
- LLM: Foundation-Sec-1.1-8B (Splunk)
- Data Source: Splunk (via MCP)
- Output: Slack SDK, HTTP webhooks
- Config: YAML + python-dotenv
- Deployment: Docker
- Testing: pytest

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/supernerve-ai/amygdala
